titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 624

div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Let present and future know that we have granted, and by this our charter made firm the resignation and quitclaim that Patrick son of Earl Patrick made to God and to Blessed Mary, and to Saint Cuthbert, and to Saint Æbbe, and to the monks of Durham serving God at Coldingham, of the toun of Swinwood with its men and just pertinents, to be held as freely and quietly as the charter of the aforesaid Patrick and the Confirmation of Earl Patrick, his father, declare, Saving our service. As witnesses: Herbert, Abbot of Kelso, Walter Oliphant, Justiciar of Lothian, Ingram de Balliol, John of Maxwell, David the Marshal, John Gifford, Bernard of Howden, Hervey the Marshal. At Roxburgh, the last day of November, the Eighth Year of our reign.
